penalty_extra_disagreement.analysis: Fifth video (V20, V22, V26, V28, V29) where Claude applies an out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty. GPT-4o's rubric-strict 3.0 is the correct rubric application. Pattern is now persistent enough to recommend prompt-level formalization of the allowed penalty set.

phase_attribution_disagreement.claude_diagnosis: Phase split: load+placement 35s, throws 205s (55+70+80), cut 135s. Cutting is the dominant loss center.
phase_attribution_disagreement.gpt4o_diagnosis: Phase split: needle_load 17s, placement 29s, first 60s, second 42s, third 205s, cut 15s. Third throw is the dominant loss center.
phase_attribution_disagreement.analysis: LARGEST phase-attribution disagreement in the series. Tails converge within 5s but the cut/third-throw boundary differs by ~120s. Same convergent-tails pattern as V21/V24/V29 but more extreme here because severe suture tangling makes the boundary between 'still tying' and 'now trimming' genuinely ambiguous. Both diagnoses point to the same overall problem: post-second-throw economy collapsed.

fls_score_reconciliation.reconciliation_note: EXACT score agreement at 227 via different math. Claude: 600 - 365 - 8 = 227. GPT-4o: 600 - 370 - 3 = 227. Most striking single-video numerical alignment in the series. The 5-second time delta and 5-point penalty delta cancel exactly. Both teachers independently identify V28 as the worst single-attempt time in the 28-video series and frame it as new-equipment/new-suture catastrophe rather than skill regression.

systematic_findings.exact_score_alignment_via_different_math: First case in the series of EXACT 0.0 FLS delta. Time delta (5s) and penalty delta (5 points) cancel precisely. Useful as a calibration anchor: when the time and penalty deltas have opposite signs and equal magnitude, the bottom line is robust even when the bookkeeping disagrees.
systematic_findings.rubric_scope_issue_recurring: Fifth case (V20, V22, V26, V28, V29) where Claude applies an out-of-rubric quality/efficiency penalty. Pattern is now persistent enough that the next prompt iteration should formalize the allowed penalty set and add a separate cosmetic_quality field to capture Claude's signal without distorting the FLS score.
systematic_findings.largest_phase_split_disagreement: Cut/third-throw boundary differs by ~120s on V28. Severe suture tangling created genuine ambiguity about when the trainee transitioned from tying to trimming. Worth flagging in future prompts: define the cut phase as starting when the cutter (scissors) first enters the field, not when the knot is 'visually seated.'
systematic_findings.drain_agreement_continues: V28 is another drain-closure agreement case. Running tally: AGREE 9 (V12, V13, V18, V19, V22, V26, V27, V28, V29), DIVERGE 5 (V11, V14, V16, V17, V20).
systematic_findings.v28_v29_v30_v31_arc_now_complete: With all four videos scored by both teachers, the new-equipment narrative is fully supported. V28 (365/370s) was a Vizio-station catastrophe. V29 (295/304.5s) was a 70s adaptation. V30 (158s, blue trainer) was a station change with immediate recovery. V31 (137s) was further adaptation. The V28 score should be read as a station-specific anomaly, not a true performance ceiling.
